reasoning: I will provide a concise summary of the article, focusing on Owain Doull's Olympic gold in the team pursuit, the world-record time, his Welsh background, and the broader context of Welsh cycling success at the Rio Games.
output: Owain Doull helped Great Britain win the 4km Olympic team pursuit in Rio, joining Bradley Wiggins, Steven Burke, and Ed Clancy to beat Australia in a world-record time of 3:50.265. The 23-year-old Welsh rider, who began cycling at Cardiff’s Maindy Fliers, called the victory surreal and a culmination of four years’ work, while Wiggins became the first Briton to win eight Olympic medals. The win adds to Wales’ growing Olympic cycling legacy since Nicole Cooke’s 2008 road race gold, with other Welsh podiums in Rio by Jazz Carlin, Victoria Thornley, and rugby sevens players Sam Cross and James Davies, and with Elinor Barker lining up for Britain's women’s pursuit gold.
